last_agent_result.risk_assessment: The regex `(thought|text)` correctly matches both channel marker patterns. All 4 call sites use extractText(message) which strips both marker types. Integration tests confirm end-to-end flow works without crashes. Fix is complete and correct.

planner.summary: Root cause identified: the `extractText()` function regex only strips `thought` channel markers (`<|channel>thought\n...<channel|>`), but the `text` channel marker pattern (`<|channel>text\n...<channel|>`) is NOT being stripped. The thinking channel works because `message.channels['thought']` returns cleanly extracted text from the API, but `message.channels['text']` returns raw output WITH markers still embedded. The regex needs to also strip `text` channel markers.
planner.problem_statement: Thinking messages display correctly with proper spaces and line breaks, but final bot response text is garbled with channel marker artifacts visible. This happens because the thinking path uses `message.channels['thought']` directly (API returns clean extracted text), while the final response uses `extractText()` which only strips thought channel markers, not text channel markers.

evidence.detail: channelMarkerRegex only matches '<|channel>thought\n[\s\S]*?<channel|>' - does NOT match text channel markers '<|channel>text\n...<channel|>'
extractText() tries channels['text']?.toString() which returns raw output WITH channel markers intact, not clean text
Thinking channel uses message.channels['thought'] directly - API extracts clean text, so onThinking receives proper content with spaces/linebreaks
Final response text uses extractText(message) which only strips thought markers, leaving text channel markers in the output
ThinkingBubble displays thinkingText.trim() correctly - thinking channel has clean text
ChatBubble for bot messages uses MarkdownText on message.text - if text has channel markers, MarkdownText will display garbled output

assumptions: The LiteRT-LM API extracts 'thought' channel content cleanly but returns raw output (with markers) for 'text' channel
The model outputs '<|channel>text\n[response content]\n<channel|>' for the final response
The text channel is not configured with custom start/end in our ConversationConfig (only 'thought' is configured)
acceptance_criteria: Final bot response text no longer contains '<|channel>text' or '<channel|>' artifacts
Final response text preserves spaces and line breaks just like thinking text does
The regex in extractText() strips both thought and text channel markers
risks: If text channel content has a different marker format, the regex may still miss some artifacts
Need to verify the exact marker format the model uses for text channel output

Verify final response displays correctly without garbled channel markers

evidence_required: No '<|channel>' or '<channel|>' strings visible in final response text

Replaced all message.toString() calls in callback handlers with extractText(message) to ensure final responses no longer contain garbled channel marker artifacts.
